signals.headline: Drift Protocol Exploit Confirmed at $280M+ Loss | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Drift Protocol Exploit Confirmed at $280M+ Loss | YES | HIGH confidence**  
On April 1, Solana's Drift Protocol suffered a $280M hack via social engineering on pre-approved transactions involving Security Council members, eroding ecosystem trust, dropping DEX volumes 40%, and pressuring SOL to $75-80 support.[4][6]

signals.headline: Solana DEX Volume Hits $117B, Surpassing Ethereum's $52B | NO | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Solana DEX Volume Hits $117B, Surpassing Ethereum's $52B | NO | MED confidence**  
Over recent weeks, Solana achieved $117 billion in DEX volume—more than double Ethereum's—amid network activity peaks like 160 million daily transactions, though both ETH ($2,039, down 12%) and SOL (down 17%) declined in price.[2]

signals.headline: Alpenglow Upgrade Passes Governance Vote with 98
signals.raw:
Alpenglow Upgrade Passes Governance Vote with 98.27% Approval | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Solana's Alpenglow upgrade, targeting 100-150ms transaction finality, secured overwhelming governance support, positioning it as a fast execution layer, yet SOL trades near $80 after a 70% peak decline with no immediate price reaction.[3]
